ESP32 pin sends power through a resistor to the LED long leg, short leg returns to ground
ESP32 GPIO27 connected to a push button that goes to GND, with internal pull-up enabled; GPIO2 drives an LED through a resistor to GND
ESP32 connected to OLED via SDA and SCL lines with shared ground, labeled 0x3C address
ESP32 GPIO27 connected to a button to GND, showing one physical press producing several fast electrical transitions before settling

ESP32 connected to BME280 and SSD1306 OLED over I2C, showing temperature, humidity, and pressure values
ESP32 I2C bus with GPIO21 SDA and GPIO22 SCL shared by an SSD1306 OLED and BME280 sensor
Three ESP32 buttons connected to GPIO25, GPIO26, and GPIO27, each with its own state and debounce timer
ESP32 connected to an SSD1306 OLED over I2C using GPIO21 SDA and GPIO22 SCL, showing ADC value on screen
ESP32 button input shown with a weak resistor pulling the GPIO to a default HIGH or LOW state
ESP32 PWM waveform showing 25 percent, 50 percent, and 75 percent duty cycles controlling LED brightness
DHT22 data line connected to ESP32 GPIO4 with 3.3 V, ground, and a pull-up resistor
ESP32 reading an LDR voltage divider on GPIO34 while light changes the ADC value
ESP32 smart environment monitor with BME280, OLED display, green LED, red LED, and push button
